chunks.page_summary: This page provides instructions on how to order WirelessHART communication for Rheonics sensors, explaining the process for both initial purchase and later upgrades, and includes a visual guide from the company's request for quotation page.
chunks.extracted_text:
Phone: +1 713 234 0863

How to order WirelessHART in Rheonics sensors?

Modified on: Wed, 11 Mar, 2026 at 9:37 PM

The purpose of this article is to explain which hardware and configuration are essential for a successful WirelessHART implementation.

1. When I order the sensor, have I already purchased WirelessHART?

No, you will need to order the WirelessHART communication protocol before the sensor is shipped. To order the WirelessHART communication protocol, remember to check the C10-WirelessHART option in the RFQ page.

WirelessHART is available on all Rheonics standard viscometers and density meters that use SME electronics. The video below shows the steps to add WirelessHART to the device configuration.

2. Can I order WirelessHART after a sensor is shipped?

Yes, communication protocols like WirelessHART can be ordered later. Contact Rheonics sales to order or go to our Order page https://rheonics.com/request-for-quotation/ (https://rheonics.com/request-for-quotation/)

[Content from screenshot of Rheonics Order Page]
rheonics.com/request-for-quotation/srv/
rheonics
SOLUTIONS PRODUCTS SUPPORT COMPANY ORDER

EZ : SME-TK : Transmitter housing with solid cover
E2 : SME-DRM : DIN-rail mount housing
E4 : SME-BOX : Desktop version of electronics with 10.4" touchscreen

FT Option - Fixed Transmitter mounted on top of sensor pro
SS Electronics - Stainless Steel Transmitter Housing, available for E1 and E2

Communication
Rheonics electronics are pre-configured with the following communication options.
C1 : 4-20 mA : 3 channels of 4-20 mA analog signal
C2 : Modbus RTU (RS-485) : Modbus RTU over RS-485
C3 : USB : USB 2.0 compliant service and data acquisition port
C4 : Ethernet : Ethernet active on RJ45 connector

Additional FieldBus Communication Options
C5 : Bluetooth LE 4.0 : Bluetooth module for short range communication (only for E1)
C6 : Modbus TCP (Article)
C7 : Ethernet/IP (Article)
C8 : HART (Article)
C9 : Profinet (Article)
C10 : WirelessHART (Article)

Temperature (select one) *
T1 : 125 °C : Sensor rated for operation in process fluids up to 125 °C (250 °F)
T2 : 150 °C : Sensor rated for operation in process fluids up to 150 °C (300 °F)

Figure 1. Ordering WirelessHART communication.

3. Cabling
key_facts: WirelessHART communication protocol must be ordered before the sensor is shipped for initial purchase.
The C10-WirelessHART option must be selected on the RFQ page to order WirelessHART.
WirelessHART is compatible with all Rheonics standard viscometers and density meters that use SME electronics.
Communication protocols like WirelessHART can be ordered after a sensor has been shipped.
Rheonics offers various communication options including 4-20 mA, Modbus RTU (RS-485), USB 2.0, Ethernet, Bluetooth LE 4.0, Modbus TCP, Ethernet/IP, HART, Profinet, and WirelessHART.
Rheonics sensors are available with temperature ratings up to 125 °C (250 °F) or 150 °C (300 °F) for process fluids.

chunks.page_summary: This page details the cost-saving benefits of WirelessHART communication, contrasting it with traditional wired installations, and provides a list of relevant Rheonics and FieldComm Group references.
chunks.extracted_text:
https://support.rheonics.com/en/support/solutions/articles/81000418933-getting-started-with-wirelesshart-communication (https://support.rheonics.com/en/support/solutions/articles/81000418933-getting-started-with-wirelesshart-communication)

4. Benefits

The "Hidden" Savings: Total Installed Cost (TIC)

This is where WirelessHART wins. In 2026, labor and material costs for wired infrastructure (conduit, cable trays, and engineering) have continued to climb.

*   Wired Installation: Can cost $60 to $120 per foot to install. If your device is 200 feet from the control room, you're looking at $12,000+ just in "getting there."
*   WirelessHART Installation: You bolt it to the pipe and turn it on. The "installation cost" is essentially just the labor for the technician to commission the device in the software.

Pro Tip:
WirelessHART is a "Mesh" network. This means the more devices you buy, the stronger and more redundant the network becomes. If you only buy one device, its signal might be weak. If you buy ten, they act as repeaters for each other.

5. References

*   Rheonics, "SRV Inline Viscometer." [Online]. Available: Rheonics SRV » inline online Viscometer for fluid viscosity monitoring (https://rheonics.com/products/inline-viscometer-srv/). [Accessed: Sep. 9, 2025].
*   Rheonics, "SRD Inline Density Meter." [Online]. Available: rheonics SRD » Density Meter inline online specific gravity concentration (https://rheonics.com/products/inline-density-meter-srd/). [Accessed: Sep. 9, 2025].
*   Rheonics, "Electronics and Communication." [Online]. Available: Electronics and Communication » rheonics :: viscometer and density meter (https://rheonics.com/electronics-and-communication/). [Accessed: Sep. 9, 2025].
*   Rheonics, "HART Fundamentals." [Online]. Available: HART Fundamentals : Rheonics Support (https://support.rheonics.com/en/support/solutions/articles/81000412287-hart-fundamentals). [Accessed: Sep. 9, 2025].
*   FieldComm Group, "WirelessHART – Expanding the Possibilities" (Rev. 3.3 brochure). [Online]. Available: WirelessHart_Expanding_Possibilities_bro_Rev3.3.pdf (https://www.fieldcommgroup.org/sites/default/files/imce_files/technology/documents/WirelessHart_Expanding_Possibilities_bro_Rev3.3.pdf). [Accessed: Sep. 9, 2025].
*   Rheonics, "Getting Started with WirelessHART Communication". [Online]. Available: Getting Started with WirelessHART Communication (https://support.rheonics.com/en/support/solutions/articles/81000418933-getting-started-with-wirelesshart-communication).
key_facts: WirelessHART communication significantly reduces Total Installed Cost (TIC) compared to wired installations.
By 2026, labor and material costs for wired infrastructure, including conduit, cable trays, and engineering, are projected to continue rising.
Wired installations can incur costs of $60 to $120 per foot.
A wired device located 200 feet from the control room could have installation costs exceeding $12,000 for infrastructure alone.
WirelessHART installation involves simple physical mounting and software commissioning.
WirelessHART utilizes a Mesh network architecture.
The strength and redundancy of a WirelessHART network increase with the number of devices.
Individual WirelessHART devices can act as repeaters, enhancing signal strength, especially when multiple devices are deployed.
All referenced online documents were accessed on September 9, 2025.
warnings_notes: Pro Tip: WirelessHART is a "Mesh" network. This means the more devices you buy, the stronger and more redundant the network becomes. If you only buy one device, its signal might be weak. If you buy ten, they act as repeaters for each other.
